title: Speaking – Part 2: Long Turn
description: Each candidate compares two of three photographs and answers two questions about them. You have about one minute. Then your partner answers a brief question.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what challenge the people might be facing and how they might be feeling.
tasks.mainQuestion: What challenge might the people be facing, and how might they be feeling?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which situation would you find most challenging?

images.label: a
images.description: A young man hiking on a steep mountain trail, looking at a map and appearing concerned, with a stormy sky above.

images.label: b
images.description: A middle-aged woman in an urban setting, holding a bicycle with a flat tire, surrounded by heavy traffic, looking exasperated.

images.label: c
images.description: A group of teenagers attempting to set up a large tent in a windy field, struggling to keep it upright, their faces showing determination.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the photographs and say what decision the people might be making and what factors they might be considering.
tasks.mainQuestion: What decision might the people be making, and what factors might they be considering?
tasks.followUpQuestion: Which decision would be hardest for you to make?

images.label: d
images.description: A couple in a modern kitchen discussing over a laptop and a catalogue, possibly deciding on new home appliances.

images.label: e
images.description: A senior man in a bookstore, surrounded by books, contemplating which book to buy, with a thoughtful expression.

images.label: f
images.description: A young woman sitting at a café table with two different job offer letters in front of her, looking deep in thought.
